construction_type: Type IIA or IIIA depending height/area and jurisdiction

parti_config.stacking_logic: public low / secure-service internal / MEP stacked

plan_archetypes: daylit perimeter reading rooms with central service core
children and noisy zones buffered from quiet reading

sources.title: Library Space: A Planning Resource for Librarians
Time-Saver Standards for Building Types — Libraries and Branch Libraries

small.name: Branch Library
small.notes: Single floor branch with children's room, meeting room, staff support

medium.name: Urban Neighborhood Library
medium.notes: Collections, digital labs, teen, community room

large.name: Main Public Library
large.notes: Central stacks, archives, multiple public rooms, maker and event spaces

space_library.name: Entry / Welcome
notes: Visual orientation, security, and self-check often align here

space_library.name: Lobby / Public Commons
notes: May include café seating, display, informal gathering

space_library.name: Collections / Stacks
notes: Mix of physical collections and browsing

space_library.name: Reading / Study Areas
notes: Quiet zone; daylight and views strongly preferred

space_library.name: Children's Library
notes: Often near family toilet and story room

space_library.name: Community Room / Meeting
notes: Should be usable after-hours with controlled access

space_library.name: Service Desk / Self Check
notes: Operational heart for visibility to entry and collections

space_library.name: Staff Workroom / Sorting
notes: Acquisitions, processing, holds, and back-office support

space_library.name: Public Restrooms
notes: Public restrooms near children's area, meeting rooms, and lobby; family/accessible restroom recommended near children's zone.
